title: oxalis hirta var. secunda
intro: Oxalis hirta var. secunda, also known as the hairy wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the oxalis genus. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and act as a natural antiseptic. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Oxalis hirta var. secunda can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in herbal medicine to promote healthy digestion and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
